name: Ronald Williams
key_contributions: Co-authored the 1986 Nature paper "Learning representations by back-propagating errors" with Rumelhart and Hinton
Invented the REINFORCE algorithm (1992) — the foundation of modern policy gradient methods in reinforcement learning
REINFORCE is a direct ancestor of the PPO algorithm used in RLHF for training ChatGPT, Claude, and other LLMs
Contributed to the mathematical foundations of training neural networks
summary: Ronald Williams is a computer scientist whose contributions span two of the most critical developments in modern AI: backpropagation and policy gradient reinforcement learning. As a co-author of the 1986 Nature paper with Rumelhart and Hinton, he helped make backpropagation practical for training multi-layer neural networks. His REINFORCE algorithm (1992) established the policy gradient framework that would eventually lead to PPO and RLHF — the techniques used to align modern LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude with human preferences.
